normative_status: non-normative unless RFC-0003 is accepted through governed review
scope: Proposed ResourceKindPolicyV1 construction and local resource unit/quantity decisions
source_basis: RFC-0003 semantic amendment candidate prepared for governed review
Frozen prior-specification oracle from isolated commit 5ea0c1b104b40e93f3896edf0d4e095d0a9de1c7, integrated as 721200c
Repository fail-closed, honest-claims, checked-arithmetic, and differential-reference requirements
assumptions: RFC-0003 is proposed input to this model and has not been accepted by this artifact.
V1 has no zero-marker permission and every policy-bound resource quantity is positive.
LifecycleNonFungible policy construction requires quantity_max exactly 1.
Non-lifecycle quantity_max 0 remains a constructible empty policy candidate.
Host width checks are representation-boundary guards outside the supplied relative semantic precedence.

remaining_ambiguities.statement: The in-place pre-alpha disposition depends on there being no canonical, persisted, activated, or externally durable resource-kind-policy identity.
remaining_ambiguities.required_resolution: Reviewers must verify and approve that precondition; any durable predecessor record requires a new schema/version and migration design.

remaining_ambiguities.statement: The supplied reason precedence has no stable protocol reject-code assignment.
remaining_ambiguities.required_resolution: Freeze codes and full boundary-versus-semantic precedence before claiming cross-language reject parity.

remaining_ambiguities.statement: EvidenceOnly remains unable to express zero marker permission or prove a nonfungible application interpretation.
remaining_ambiguities.required_resolution: Keep quantities positive in v1 and define any marker or evidence-semantics extension in a versioned future schema.

remaining_ambiguities.statement: RFC-0003 selects an in-place draft-v1 amendment under the no-durable-policy precondition, but this artifact cannot approve that governance choice.
remaining_ambiguities.required_resolution: Obtain explicit human and required independent approval before promotion or authority integration.

findings.title: Lifecycle maximum zero is removed from policy construction
attacker_capabilities: Submit an inert lifecycle policy candidate with a chosen maximum
preconditions: Schema and validity window are valid
findings.invariant: Under the proposal, every constructed LifecycleNonFungible policy has quantity_max exactly 1.
findings.formal_or_property_obligation: Proposed lifecycle policy acceptance implies quantity_max == 1.
findings.residual_risk: This is a compatibility-breaking acceptance-set removal if prior lifecycle policy candidates have durable identity.

findings.title: Lifecycle maximum above one is removed from policy construction
attacker_capabilities: Submit an inert lifecycle policy candidate with an otherwise valid maximum
preconditions: Schema and validity window are valid
findings.invariant: Under the proposal, lifecycle construction rejects every maximum other than 1.
findings.formal_or_property_obligation: For every typed u128 m, proposed lifecycle construction accepts exactly when m == 1, subject to earlier schema and validity acceptance.
findings.residual_risk: Migration and predecessor-policy handling remain undefined until RFC-0003 decides them.

findings.title: Unsupported schema precedes validity and lifecycle maximum
attacker_capabilities: Submit a candidate with multiple observable defects
findings.invariant: Constructor semantic precedence begins with supported schema.
findings.formal_or_property_obligation: Any unsupported schema reports unsupported_schema regardless of later validity or lifecycle-maximum defects.

findings.title: Invalid validity window precedes lifecycle maximum
attacker_capabilities: Submit a supported-schema candidate with multiple semantic defects
findings.invariant: After schema acceptance, validity-window failure precedes lifecycle-maximum failure.
findings.formal_or_property_obligation: For supported typed candidates, an invalid validity window reports before lifecycle maximum inequality.
findings.residual_risk: Width-failure placement still requires a complete stable-code specification.

findings.title: Non-lifecycle maximum zero remains constructible
attacker_capabilities: Submit an inert non-lifecycle policy candidate with maximum zero
preconditions: Schema and validity window are valid
findings.invariant: RFC-0003 changes lifecycle construction only; non-lifecycle maximum zero remains an empty candidate.

expected_result.non_claim: an admitted resource may not exist

findings.formal_or_property_obligation: For every non-lifecycle mode, maximum zero remains constructible and admits no positive quantity.
findings.residual_risk: Authenticated activation and operational disablement meaning are outside this oracle.

findings.title: Unit mismatch precedes lifecycle and zero failures
attacker_capabilities: Choose both a mismatched unit and an invalid lifecycle quantity
preconditions: The proposed lifecycle policy constructs with unit unit-u and maximum 1
The resource uses unit-v and quantity 0
findings.invariant: Resource reason precedence begins with exact UnitId equality.
findings.formal_or_property_obligation: A unit mismatch reports before lifecycle exact-one, zero, or maximum for a constructed typed policy.
findings.residual_risk: Conversions still require separately authorized transformation semantics.

findings.title: Lifecycle exact-one precedes general zero
findings.boundary: Proposed lifecycle resource semantic reason selection
attacker_capabilities: Submit zero quantity under a constructed lifecycle policy
findings.invariant: After unit equality, lifecycle exact-one reports before the general positive-quantity rule.
findings.formal_or_property_obligation: For a constructed lifecycle policy and matching unit, every quantity other than 1 reports lifecycle_quantity_must_equal_one.
findings.residual_risk: This is a reason-only change for quantity zero; stable code assignment remains pending.

findings.title: Lifecycle exact-one precedes general maximum
findings.boundary: Proposed lifecycle resource semantic reason selection
attacker_capabilities: Submit an over-maximum quantity under a constructed lifecycle policy
findings.invariant: After unit equality, lifecycle exact-one reports before the general maximum.
findings.formal_or_property_obligation: For a constructed lifecycle policy and matching unit, maximum failure never masks exact-one failure.
findings.residual_risk: This is a reason-only change for quantity 2; stable code assignment remains pending.

findings.title: EvidenceOnly still has no zero-marker permission
attacker_capabilities: Select EvidenceOnly and submit a zero-quantity resource
findings.invariant: Every v1 policy-bound resource quantity is positive because no marker permission exists.
findings.formal_or_property_obligation: For every proposed-constructible policy, resource acceptance implies quantity_atoms > 0.
findings.residual_risk: Positive EvidenceOnly quantity still cannot prove nonmonetary or nonfungible application meaning.

findings.title: The resource acceptance delta follows lifecycle policy narrowing
findings.boundary: Differential composition of policy construction and resource validation
attacker_capabilities: Submit a baseline-valid lifecycle policy candidate and resource
findings.invariant: RFC-0003 removes resource states whose lifecycle policy no longer constructs and adds no new accepted state.
findings.formal_or_property_obligation: Across the bounded differential domain, the only removed resource acceptances are lifecycle maximum greater than 1 with quantity 1; no acceptance is added.
findings.residual_risk: The unbounded equivalence still requires proof or a reviewed refinement argument after RFC acceptance.

findings.title: The non-lifecycle u128 maximum remains accepted exactly
attacker_capabilities: Submit the largest representable policy and resource quantity
findings.invariant: RFC-0003 introduces no narrowing or non-lifecycle maximum regression.
findings.formal_or_property_obligation: Every non-lifecycle structural decision at u128::MAX matches the frozen baseline.
findings.residual_risk: Transition-level sums still require checked arithmetic and may overflow.
